'和歌山県和歌山市吉礼', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:都麻都比売神社]] {{wikidata link|Q17216259}} == Japanese content == {{List of coordinates}} '''Tsumatsuhime Shrine'' is a {{jalink|shikiuchi shrine}} ({{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964}}) in {{jalink|Kiokuni|Q864053}}{{jalink|Nakusa District|Q11415547}}. === Overview === {{jalink|extension (Era name)|Q1327274|Extended}} "{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}" established in 5 years ({{jalink|927 years|Q30470}}) {{jalin k|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|Shinmyocho}}, {{jalink|Kiokuni|Q864053}}{{jalink|Nakusa District|Q11415547}} "Tomatohime Shrine Meishin University The following three shrines in {{jalink|Wakayama Prefecture|Q131314}}{{jalink|Wakayama City|Q200747}} are said to be {{jalink|Ronsha|Q135022904}} in {{jalink|Shikiuchi}} ({{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964}}), which are described as ``Monthly New Year''. # '''{{jalink|#Miyakomatsuhime Shrine (Kichirei, Wakayama City)||Miyakomatsuhime Shrine}}''' (Kichirei, Wakayama City) - Former {{jalink|Murasha|Q134917284}}. # '''{{jalink|#Tomatohime Shrine (Hirao, Wakayama City)||Tomatohime Shrine}}''' (Hirao, Wakayama City) # '''{{jalink|Takazumi Shrine|Q17226307|Takazumi Shrine}}''' (Wakayama City Negi, {{Wiki coordinates|34|14|5.04|N|135|15|41.42|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|location|name=Takazumi Shrine}}) - It is also {{jalink|Ronsha|Q135022904}} of Shikinaisha ``Takazumi Hime Shrine'' and ``Takazumi Hime Shrine.'' Old village company. → See {{jalink|Takazumi Shrine|Q17226307}}. === Enshrined God ===
'''Abbreviated genealogy of the three Ita-kei gods'' (written according to the Nihon Shoki) {{familytree/start|style=font-size:100%}} {{familytree|border=0||||01| 01={{jalink|Susano-o|Q272993|Susa no Takashi}}
({{jalink|Susa Shrine (Arita City)|Q11664501|Susa Shrine}})}} {{familytree|border=0|||,|-|+|-|-|.| }}{{familytree|border=0|01|02|03| 01={{jalink|50 Fierce God|Q10881556|50 Fierce Life}}
({{jalink|Itaukeso Shrine|Q11379271}})|02={{jalink|Ooyatsuhime Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|{{color|#FC4E6B|Oyatsuhime no Mikoto}}}}
({{jalink|Oyatsuhime Shrine|Q11434556|Oyatsuhime Shrine}})|03='''{{jalink|Ooyatsuhime Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|{{color|#FC4E6B|Hamatsuhime no Mikoto}}}}'''
('''Miyatsuhime Shrine''')}} {{familytree/end}}
As the company name suggests, it is said to be a shrine that enshrines '''{{jalink|Ooyatsuhime/Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|Tsumatsuhime}}''' (Tsumatsuhime no Mikoto, Sakutsuhime no Mikoto) as its enshrined deity.Regarding Tsumatsuhime, it is described in one book in "{{jalink|Nihon Shoki|Q813935}}" that she is the daughter god of {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoo}}. Also, the older brother god of Tsumatsuhime is {{jalink|Isotakejin|Q10881556|Isotakeru/Itakeru}}, and the older sister god is {{jalink|Ooyatsuhime/Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|Oyatsuhime no Mikoto}} ( Both gods are enshrined at {{jalink|Itaikiso Shrine|Q11379271}} (Itaikiso, Wakayama City) and {{jalink|Oyatsuhime Shrine|Q11434556}} (Udamori, Wakayama City), respectively. These three deities are said to be the gods who brought tree species to Kii Province, and in Kii they are collectively known as ``''Itaiki Sosanjin'' {{Sfn|Takazumi Shrine (Taira)|1983}}. === History === ==== Outline ==== The first reference to the historical material is the article in "{{jalink|Shoku Nihongi|Q611399}}" {{jalink|Taiho (Japan)|Q1346269|Taiho}} 2nd year ({{jalink|702|Q23497}})"Shoku Nihongi" article dated February 22, 702. In , there is an article about the separation of the three shrines of Ita-Kiso, Oya-Tohime, and Toma-Tohime.{{Sfn|Takazumi Shrine (Taira)|1983}}. "Shinsho Kaku Chofusho" {{jalink|Daido (Japan)|Q1373191|Daido}} 1st year ({{jalink|806|Q30021}})"Shinsho Kakuchofusho" 10 (Shinji Shoke Fudo) Daido 1st year (806). In , 13 houses were given to ``Tomazuhime no Kami'' of Kii Province {{Sfn|Takazumi Shrine (Taira)|1983}}. Related, {{jalink|Shohei (Japan)|Q1324874|Shohei}} ({{jalink|931|Q24789}}-{{jalink|938|Q24794}}) "{{jalink|Japanese name Ruijusho|Q2702 542}}', ``Tsuma (Tsuma) Go'' is seen in Kii Province {{jalink|Nakusa District|Q11415547}}, which is said to mean ``Tsuma Kobe'' {{Sfn|Takazumi Shrine (Taira)|1983}}.As {{jalink|Divine|Q11591025}}, {{jalink|Jōkan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jōkan}} In the first year of the year ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), he was promoted to the lower rank of Jushii (junior fourth rank) as ``Shinto Matsuhime'' along with Itagesojin and Ōyatsuhime no kami {{Sfn|Takazumi Shrine (Taira)|1983}}. {{jalink|Extension (era name)|Q1327274|Extension}} 5 years ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}) "{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}" {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|Shinmyocho}} states that in Nagusa District, Kii Province, there are "Tomatohime Shrine Meishindai It will be listed at {{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964}} as ``Monthly New Year'' and {{jalink|Monthly Festival|Q11516161}} and {{jalink| It is written that it was kept in {{jalink|heihaku|Q11481885}} at Niinamesai|Q11501518}}{{Sfn|Takazumi Shrine (Taira)|1983}}. In ``{{jalink|Kiokuni Shinmecho|Q17210008}}'', the Tenjin is listed as ``Junior Fourth Rank Upper Tumatsu Hime Okami'', and it is different from Itaukeso Okami, who rose to Shoichi Rank (Shoichi Rank), and has an open shrine {{Sfn|Takazumi Shrine (Taira)|1983}}. In addition, in the same book, there is also a related entry of ``Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Tsumutohime no Okami.'' Its subsequent history is unclear, and there are various theories as to which shrine the above shrine belongs to. ==== God level ==== * Records of {{jalink|Divine rank|Q11591025}} dedication in the history of the Six Kingdoms ** {{jalink|Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jogan}} On the 27th of the first month of the first year ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), the rank of Jufifth Rank, Lower Rank, Eighth Grade to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Rank, Eighth Grade (``{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}'') - The notation is ``Shinto Matsuhime God''. * After Six Kingdoms History ** Upper Juichii (``{{jalink|Kiokuni Shinto Name Book|Q17210008}}'') - Written as ``Tomato Hime Okami.'' Is it a typo in "Junior Fourth Rank Upper"? === Historical evidence === The exact location of Shikinai Shrine ``Tomatsu Hime Shrine'' is still uncertain. In ancient times, it is identified as Miyako Matsuhime Shrine (Kichirei, (1)) in Shandong in the "Kiokuni Famous Places Zue" and "Nanki Shrine Record" ({{jalink|Enkyo|Q1183728}}3 ({{jalink|1746|Q7589}})). Furthermore, in ``{{jalink|Kii Zoku Fudoki|Q11604964}}'', it is said that Matsuhime Shrine (1), the capital of Shandong, which is mentioned in ``Nanki Shrine Roku'', was originally dedicated to ``Yoshire Tsuhime no Mikoto'' {{Sfn|Takazumi Shrine (Taira)|1983}}. Yoshiretsuhime-no-Mikoto appears in the ``Kiokuni Jinmyocho'' as ``Junior Fifth Rank Lower, Yoshiretsu Hime-no-Kami.'' In addition, in "Zoku Fudoki", there is a theory that Hirao's wife Daimyo Shrine (currently Tomatohime Shrine, (2)) is referred to as ``Toumatsuhime Shrine'' {{Sfn|Takazumi Shrine (Taira)|1983}}. One of these theories is that {{jalink|Water theory|Q2464727}} ({{jalink|Water theory of Szene-Kunikake Shrine and Kodaimyojin|Q11504966}}) that occurred between {{jalink|Maezen Shrine/Kokukake Shrine|Q888184}} and Wasaso. |Q1193059}}5 ({{jalink|1433|Q6356}}) states that Takazumi Shrine (3) was originally located in Maemiya, and later moved its enshrined place to Shandong, and later to Mt. Wasa.{{Sfn|Takazumi Shrine (Taira)|1983}}. Since this legend is consistent with an article written in 702 in the ``Shoku Nihongi'', ``Zoku Fudoki'' states that there is a high possibility that Takazumi Shrine is Tomatohime Shrine {{Sfn|Takazumi Shrine (Taira)|1983}}. === Miyakomatsuhime Shrine (Yoshire, Wakayama City) === {{Shrine |Name = Miyakomatsuhime Shrine |Image = [[File:Tsumatsuhime-jinja (Kire) shaden.JPG|280px]]
Shrine building |Location = {{jalink|Wakayama Prefecture|Q131314}}{{jalink|Wakayama City|Q200747}}Yoshire 911 |Position = {{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|34|12|18.49|N|135|14|6.79|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|name=Miyakomatsuhime Shrine (Kichire)}} |Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Ooyatsuhime/Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|Miyakomatsuhime no Mikoto}} |Divinity = |Shakaku = {{jalink|Shikiuchisha}} ({{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|Meishindai}}) {{jalink|Ronsha|Q135022904}}
Old {{jalink|Murasha|Q134917284}} |Foundation = unknown |Main shrine = {{jalink|Ryuzo|Q210684}} |alias= |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular Festival = {{jalink|October 14th|Q2922}} |Shinto ritual = Red bean porridge ritual ({{jalink|January 15th|Q2260}}) |Map = {{Location map|Japan Wakayama|label=Miyakomatsuhime
Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|34|12|18.49|N|135|14|6.79|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|12|18.49|N|135|14|6.79|E}}}} }} '''Tsumatsuhime Shrine'' (Tsumatsuhime Shrine) is located in {{jalink|Wakayama Prefecture|Q131314}}{{jalink|Wakayama City|Q200747}}Yoshire. {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964}}) {{jalink|Ronsha|Q135022904}}, {{jalink|Modern shrine rank system|Q712534|Old shrine rank}} is {{jalink|Murasha|Q134917284}}. === Enshrined God === '''Main enshrined deity''' * {{jalink|Ooyatsuhime・Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|Miyakomatsuhime no Mikoto}} * Aiden: Yoshiretsuhime no Mikoto '''Deity enshrined'' * Left Hall: {{jalink|Goju Takekami|Q10881556|Goju Takekami}} * Right shrine: {{jalink|Ooyatsuhime Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|Oyatsuhime no Mikoto}} === History === {{jalink|Kiokuni|Q864053}}{{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964}} "Tomatsu Hime Shrine" in {{jalink|Nakusa District|Q11415547}} {{jalink|Ronsha|Q135022904}}. This is compared to our company in "Ki Province Meisho Zue" and "Nanki Shrine Record" ({{jalink|Enkyo|Q1183728}}3 ({{jalink|1746|Q7589}})). On the other hand, "Zoku Fudoki" denies this, and our company says that it is a shrine that enshrines the "Junior Fifth Rank Lower Yoshiretsuhime-no-kami" that appears in the "Kiokuni Jinmyocho", and compares Matsumatsuhime-kami to Takazumi Shrine. According to the shrine statement book, our company was dedicated as Shikinai Shrine ``Miyatomatsuhime Shrine'' during the Edo period, but in {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} it was renamed to ``Yoshiretsuhime Shrine'' {{Sfn|Miyatomatsu Hime Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987|p=51-52}}. In the 6th year of the Meiji era ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}), it was ranked as {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} in the {{jalink|modern shrine rank system|Q712534|old shrine rank}}. After that, in {{jalink|Showa|Q327567}}21 ({{jalink|1946|Q18610}}), it reverted to its former name ``Miyakomatsuhime Shrine'' and remains the current name. === Setsusha === * The description of Inari Shrine Setsusue Shrine is from [http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=1053 Miyakomatsuhime Shrine (Kichire)] (Wakayama Prefectural Shrine Agency). * Benzaitensha * Gionsha * Goshusha * Kawagozensha * Goryosha * Sofusha File:Tsumatsuhime-jinja (Kire) keidai.JPG|Precincts File:Tsumatsuhime-jinja (Kire) torii.JPG|Torii === Miyamato Hime Shrine (Hirao, Wakayama City) === {{Shrine |Name = Tomatsu Hime Shrine |Image = [[File:Tsumatsuhime-jinja (Hirao) shaden.JPG|280px]]
Shrine building |Location = {{jalink|Wakayama Prefecture|Q131314}}{{jalink|Wakayama City|Q200747}}Hirao Aza Wakabayashi 957 |Position = {{Wiki coordinates 2 degrees, minutes, seconds|34|12|30.30|N|135|14|49.90|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|name=Tomato Hime Shrine (Hirao)}} |Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Ooyatsuhime/Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|Miyakomatsuhime no Mikoto}} |Divinity = |Shakaku = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|Meishin University}}) {{jalink|Ronsha|Q135022904}} |Foundation = unknown |Main shrine = {{jalink|Ryuzo|Q210684}} |alias= |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular Festival = First Pig Day in October {{Sfn|Tomato Hime Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987|p=51-52}} |Shinto ritual = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Wakayama|label=Miyakomatsuhime
Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|34|12|30.30|N|135|14|49.90|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|12|30.30|N|135|14|49.90|E}}}} }} '''Tsumatsu Hime Shrine'' (Tsumatsu Hime Jinja, Miyako Mazu Hime Shrine) is located in {{jalink|Wakayama Prefecture|Q131314}}{{jalink|Wakayama City|Q200747}}Hirao. {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964}}) {{jalink|Ronsha|Q135022904}}. {{Sfn|Tomatsu Hime Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine) |1987|p=51-52}} adjacent to the ruins of Heioji Shrine on the Kumano Kodo. === Enshrined God === * {{jalink|Ooyatsuhime・Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|Miyakomatsuhime no Mikoto}} === History === {{jalink|Kiokuni|Q864053}}{{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964}} "Tomatsu Hime Shrine" in {{jalink|Nakusa District|Q11415547}} {{jalink|Ronsha|Q135022904}}. In ``Zoku Fudoki'', there is a theory that compares the company to this, and in addition, there is a theory that compares it to ``Junior Fourth Rank Lower, Tsumatsuhiba Okami'' in the ``Kiokuni Jinmyocho'' {{Sfn|Hirao Village (Taira)|1983}}. The company name is ``Tsumadaimyo Shrine'' in ``Zoku Fudoki,'' and ``Tsuma Gozen Shrine'' in ``Kiokuni Meisho Zue.'' According to "Zoku Fudoki", the precincts were once two and a half towns and the shrine building was majestic, but it fell into disrepair during the Tensho War, and although it was revived by {{jalink|Toyotomi Hidenaga|Q1191183}}, it declined. Currently, a small shrine is enshrined in the precincts of about 6 steps. Two stone lanterns dating back to {{jalink|Original|Q1145754}}5 ({{jalink|1740|Q7006}}) stand in the shrine grounds. Furthermore, according to "Kanei-ki", it is said that on the first day of the New Year, the first day of the Pig in October, and the first day of the New Year in November, members of the shrine of Ita-keiso Shrine once served the company. === Takazumi Shrine === {{See|Takazumi Shrine}} === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} '''Notes''' {{reflist|group="Note"}} '''Original source''': Introducing the primary historical sources for the items mentioned. {{reflist|group="hara"}} '''Source''' {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor=Edited by Shikinaisha Research Group|author=|chapter=|year=1987|title=Shikiuchisha Research Report Volume 23 Nankaido|publisher={{jalink|Kogakukan University|Q1797584}}Publishing Department|page=|isbn=|ref=}} ** {{Wikicite|reference=Suzuo Oku "Tomatsu Hime Shrine" |ref={{Harvid|Tomatsu Hime Shrine (Shikinai) | 1987}}}}, {{Wikicite|reference=Sadashi Nakamura "Takazumi Hiko Shrine/Takazumi Hime Shrine" |ref={{Harvid|Takazumi Hime Shrine/Takazumi Hime Shrine (Shikinai) | 1987}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=|title=Japanese Historical Place Name System 31 Place Names of Wakayama Prefecture|publisher={{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}|isbn=9784582490312|ref=}} ** {{Wikicite|reference="Kichire Village"|ref={{Harvid|Kichire Village (Taira)|1983}}}}, {{Wikicite|reference="Hirao Village"|ref={{Harvid|Hirao Village (Taira)|1983}}}}, {{Wikicite|reference="Takazumi Shrine"|ref={{Harvid|Takazumi Shrine (Taira)|1983}}}} === Related items === * {{jalink|Ooyatsuhime/Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047}} * Deities related to Kii capital Mato Hime no Mikoto ** {{jalink|Susa Shrine (Arita City)|Q11664501|Susa Shrine}} ({{jalink|Arita City|Q657843}} Senda, {{Wiki coordinates|34|3|53.55|N|135|8|29.32|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|location|name=Susa Shrine (Father God)}}) - The father god of Tsuma Tsuhime. ** {{jalink|Ita-Kiso Shrine|Q11379271}} (Ita-Kiso, Wakayama City, {{Wiki coordinates|34|12|6.17|N|135|14|59.77|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|location|name=Ita-Kiso Shrine (older brother)}}) - The older brother of Tsuma Tsuhime. ** {{jalink|Oyatsuhime Shrine|Q11434556}} (Udamori, Wakayama City, {{Wiki coordinates|34|15|39.59|N|135|14|59.02|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|location|name=Oyatsuhime Shrine (sister deity)}}) - Sister deity of Tomatsuhime. === External link === {{Commonscat|Tsumatsuhime-jinja (Kire)}} * [http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=1053 Miyakomatsuhime Shrine (Yoshire)], [http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=1051 Miyamato Hime Shrine (Hirao)], [http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=1034 Takazumi Shrine] (Wakayama Prefectural Shrine Agency) * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/520402.html Miyamato Hime Shrine] (Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto/Shrine Historical Materials Collection”) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsumatsuhime Shinsiya}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsumatsuhime Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsumatsuhime Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kii Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:和歌山市の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:紀伊国の式内社|名つまつひめしんしや]] [[Category:和歌山県の旧村社]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:kkkkkkkkkkkkkk]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qq]]

As the company name suggests, it is said to be a shrine that enshrines '''{{jalink|Ooyatsuhime/Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|Tsumatsuhime}}''' (Tsumatsuhime no Mikoto, Sakutsuhime no Mikoto) as its enshrined deity.Regarding Tsumatsuhime, it is described in one book in "{{jalink|Nihon Shoki|Q813935}}" that she is the daughter god of {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoo}}. Also, the older brother god of Tsumatsuhime is {{jalink|Isotakejin|Q10881556|Isotakeru/Itakeru}}, and the older sister god is {{jalink|Ooyatsuhime/Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|Oyatsuhime no Mikoto}} ( Both gods are enshrined at {{jalink|Itaikiso Shrine|Q11379271}} (Itaikiso, Wakayama City) and {{jalink|Oyatsuhime Shrine|Q11434556}} (Udamori, Wakayama City), respectively. These three deities are said to be the gods who brought tree species to Kii Province, and in Kii they are collectively known as ``''Itaiki Sosanjin'' {{Sfn|Takazumi Shrine (Taira)|1983}}. === History === ==== Outline ==== The first reference to the historical material is the article in "{{jalink|Shoku Nihongi|Q611399}}" {{jalink|Taiho (Japan)|Q1346269|Taiho}} 2nd year ({{jalink|702|Q23497}})"Shoku Nihongi" article dated February 22, 702. In , there is an article about the separation of the three shrines of Ita-Kiso, Oya-Tohime, and Toma-Tohime.{{Sfn|Takazumi Shrine (Taira)|1983}}. "Shinsho Kaku Chofusho" {{jalink|Daido (Japan)|Q1373191|Daido}} 1st year ({{jalink|806|Q30021}})"Shinsho Kakuchofusho" 10 (Shinji Shoke Fudo) Daido 1st year (806). In , 13 houses were given to ``Tomazuhime no Kami'' of Kii Province {{Sfn|Takazumi Shrine (Taira)|1983}}. Related, {{jalink|Shohei (Japan)|Q1324874|Shohei}} ({{jalink|931|Q24789}}-{{jalink|938|Q24794}}) "{{jalink|Japanese name Ruijusho|Q2702 542}}', ``Tsuma (Tsuma) Go'' is seen in Kii Province {{jalink|Nakusa District|Q11415547}}, which is said to mean ``Tsuma Kobe'' {{Sfn|Takazumi Shrine (Taira)|1983}}.As {{jalink|Divine|Q11591025}}, {{jalink|Jōkan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jōkan}} In the first year of the year ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), he was promoted to the lower rank of Jushii (junior fourth rank) as ``Shinto Matsuhime'' along with Itagesojin and Ōyatsuhime no kami {{Sfn|Takazumi Shrine (Taira)|1983}}. {{jalink|Extension (era name)|Q1327274|Extension}} 5 years ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}) "{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}" {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|Shinmyocho}} states that in Nagusa District, Kii Province, there are "Tomatohime Shrine Meishindai It will be listed at {{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964}} as ``Monthly New Year'' and {{jalink|Monthly Festival|Q11516161}} and {{jalink| It is written that it was kept in {{jalink|heihaku|Q11481885}} at Niinamesai|Q11501518}}{{Sfn|Takazumi Shrine (Taira)|1983}}. In ``{{jalink|Kiokuni Shinmecho|Q17210008}}'', the Tenjin is listed as ``Junior Fourth Rank Upper Tumatsu Hime Okami'', and it is different from Itaukeso Okami, who rose to Shoichi Rank (Shoichi Rank), and has an open shrine {{Sfn|Takazumi Shrine (Taira)|1983}}. In addition, in the same book, there is also a related entry of ``Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Tsumutohime no Okami.'' Its subsequent history is unclear, and there are various theories as to which shrine the above shrine belongs to. ==== God level ==== * Records of {{jalink|Divine rank|Q11591025}} dedication in the history of the Six Kingdoms ** {{jalink|Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jogan}} On the 27th of the first month of the first year ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), the rank of Jufifth Rank, Lower Rank, Eighth Grade to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Rank, Eighth Grade (``{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}'') - The notation is ``Shinto Matsuhime God''. * After Six Kingdoms History ** Upper Juichii (``{{jalink|Kiokuni Shinto Name Book|Q17210008}}'') - Written as ``Tomato Hime Okami.'' Is it a typo in "Junior Fourth Rank Upper"? === Historical evidence === The exact location of Shikinai Shrine ``Tomatsu Hime Shrine'' is still uncertain. In ancient times, it is identified as Miyako Matsuhime Shrine (Kichirei, (1)) in Shandong in the "Kiokuni Famous Places Zue" and "Nanki Shrine Record" ({{jalink|Enkyo|Q1183728}}3 ({{jalink|1746|Q7589}})). Furthermore, in ``{{jalink|Kii Zoku Fudoki|Q11604964}}'', it is said that Matsuhime Shrine (1), the capital of Shandong, which is mentioned in ``Nanki Shrine Roku'', was originally dedicated to ``Yoshire Tsuhime no Mikoto'' {{Sfn|Takazumi Shrine (Taira)|1983}}. Yoshiretsuhime-no-Mikoto appears in the ``Kiokuni Jinmyocho'' as ``Junior Fifth Rank Lower, Yoshiretsu Hime-no-Kami.'' In addition, in "Zoku Fudoki", there is a theory that Hirao's wife Daimyo Shrine (currently Tomatohime Shrine, (2)) is referred to as ``Toumatsuhime Shrine'' {{Sfn|Takazumi Shrine (Taira)|1983}}. One of these theories is that {{jalink|Water theory|Q2464727}} ({{jalink|Water theory of Szene-Kunikake Shrine and Kodaimyojin|Q11504966}}) that occurred between {{jalink|Maezen Shrine/Kokukake Shrine|Q888184}} and Wasaso. |Q1193059}}5 ({{jalink|1433|Q6356}}) states that Takazumi Shrine (3) was originally located in Maemiya, and later moved its enshrined place to Shandong, and later to Mt. Wasa.{{Sfn|Takazumi Shrine (Taira)|1983}}. Since this legend is consistent with an article written in 702 in the ``Shoku Nihongi'', ``Zoku Fudoki'' states that there is a high possibility that Takazumi Shrine is Tomatohime Shrine {{Sfn|Takazumi Shrine (Taira)|1983}}. === Miyakomatsuhime Shrine (Yoshire, Wakayama City) === {{Shrine |Name = Miyakomatsuhime Shrine |Image = [[File:Tsumatsuhime-jinja (Kire) shaden.JPG|280px]]
Shrine building |Location = {{jalink|Wakayama Prefecture|Q131314}}{{jalink|Wakayama City|Q200747}}Yoshire 911 |Position = {{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|34|12|18.49|N|135|14|6.79|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|name=Miyakomatsuhime Shrine (Kichire)}} |Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Ooyatsuhime/Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|Miyakomatsuhime no Mikoto}} |Divinity = |Shakaku = {{jalink|Shikiuchisha}} ({{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|Meishindai}}) {{jalink|Ronsha|Q135022904}}

Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|34|12|18.49|N|135|14|6.79|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|12|18.49|N|135|14|6.79|E}}}} }} '''Tsumatsuhime Shrine'' (Tsumatsuhime Shrine) is located in {{jalink|Wakayama Prefecture|Q131314}}{{jalink|Wakayama City|Q200747}}Yoshire. {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964}}) {{jalink|Ronsha|Q135022904}}, {{jalink|Modern shrine rank system|Q712534|Old shrine rank}} is {{jalink|Murasha|Q134917284}}. === Enshrined God === '''Main enshrined deity''' * {{jalink|Ooyatsuhime・Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|Miyakomatsuhime no Mikoto}} * Aiden: Yoshiretsuhime no Mikoto '''Deity enshrined'' * Left Hall: {{jalink|Goju Takekami|Q10881556|Goju Takekami}} * Right shrine: {{jalink|Ooyatsuhime Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|Oyatsuhime no Mikoto}} === History === {{jalink|Kiokuni|Q864053}}{{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964}} "Tomatsu Hime Shrine" in {{jalink|Nakusa District|Q11415547}} {{jalink|Ronsha|Q135022904}}. This is compared to our company in "Ki Province Meisho Zue" and "Nanki Shrine Record" ({{jalink|Enkyo|Q1183728}}3 ({{jalink|1746|Q7589}})). On the other hand, "Zoku Fudoki" denies this, and our company says that it is a shrine that enshrines the "Junior Fifth Rank Lower Yoshiretsuhime-no-kami" that appears in the "Kiokuni Jinmyocho", and compares Matsumatsuhime-kami to Takazumi Shrine. According to the shrine statement book, our company was dedicated as Shikinai Shrine ``Miyatomatsuhime Shrine'' during the Edo period, but in {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} it was renamed to ``Yoshiretsuhime Shrine'' {{Sfn|Miyatomatsu Hime Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987|p=51-52}}. In the 6th year of the Meiji era ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}), it was ranked as {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} in the {{jalink|modern shrine rank system|Q712534|old shrine rank}}. After that, in {{jalink|Showa|Q327567}}21 ({{jalink|1946|Q18610}}), it reverted to its former name ``Miyakomatsuhime Shrine'' and remains the current name. === Setsusha === * The description of Inari Shrine Setsusue Shrine is from [http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=1053 Miyakomatsuhime Shrine (Kichire)] (Wakayama Prefectural Shrine Agency). * Benzaitensha * Gionsha * Goshusha * Kawagozensha * Goryosha * Sofusha File:Tsumatsuhime-jinja (Kire) keidai.JPG|Precincts File:Tsumatsuhime-jinja (Kire) torii.JPG|Torii === Miyamato Hime Shrine (Hirao, Wakayama City) === {{Shrine |Name = Tomatsu Hime Shrine |Image = [[File:Tsumatsuhime-jinja (Hirao) shaden.JPG|280px]]
Shrine building |Location = {{jalink|Wakayama Prefecture|Q131314}}{{jalink|Wakayama City|Q200747}}Hirao Aza Wakabayashi 957 |Position = {{Wiki coordinates 2 degrees, minutes, seconds|34|12|30.30|N|135|14|49.90|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|name=Tomato Hime Shrine (Hirao)}} |Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Ooyatsuhime/Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|Miyakomatsuhime no Mikoto}} |Divinity = |Shakaku = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|Meishin University}}) {{jalink|Ronsha|Q135022904}} |Foundation = unknown |Main shrine = {{jalink|Ryuzo|Q210684}} |alias= |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular Festival = First Pig Day in October {{Sfn|Tomato Hime Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987|p=51-52}} |Shinto ritual = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Wakayama|label=Miyakomatsuhime
Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|34|12|30.30|N|135|14|49.90|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|12|30.30|N|135|14|49.90|E}}}} }} '''Tsumatsu Hime Shrine'' (Tsumatsu Hime Jinja, Miyako Mazu Hime Shrine) is located in {{jalink|Wakayama Prefecture|Q131314}}{{jalink|Wakayama City|Q200747}}Hirao. {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964}}) {{jalink|Ronsha|Q135022904}}. {{Sfn|Tomatsu Hime Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine) |1987|p=51-52}} adjacent to the ruins of Heioji Shrine on the Kumano Kodo. === Enshrined God === * {{jalink|Ooyatsuhime・Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|Miyakomatsuhime no Mikoto}} === History === {{jalink|Kiokuni|Q864053}}{{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964}} "Tomatsu Hime Shrine" in {{jalink|Nakusa District|Q11415547}} {{jalink|Ronsha|Q135022904}}. In ``Zoku Fudoki'', there is a theory that compares the company to this, and in addition, there is a theory that compares it to ``Junior Fourth Rank Lower, Tsumatsuhiba Okami'' in the ``Kiokuni Jinmyocho'' {{Sfn|Hirao Village (Taira)|1983}}. The company name is ``Tsumadaimyo Shrine'' in ``Zoku Fudoki,'' and ``Tsuma Gozen Shrine'' in ``Kiokuni Meisho Zue.'' According to "Zoku Fudoki", the precincts were once two and a half towns and the shrine building was majestic, but it fell into disrepair during the Tensho War, and although it was revived by {{jalink|Toyotomi Hidenaga|Q1191183}}, it declined. Currently, a small shrine is enshrined in the precincts of about 6 steps. Two stone lanterns dating back to {{jalink|Original|Q1145754}}5 ({{jalink|1740|Q7006}}) stand in the shrine grounds. Furthermore, according to "Kanei-ki", it is said that on the first day of the New Year, the first day of the Pig in October, and the first day of the New Year in November, members of the shrine of Ita-keiso Shrine once served the company. === Takazumi Shrine === {{See|Takazumi Shrine}} === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} '''Notes''' {{reflist|group="Note"}} '''Original source''': Introducing the primary historical sources for the items mentioned. {{reflist|group="hara"}} '''Source''' {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor=Edited by Shikinaisha Research Group|author=|chapter=|year=1987|title=Shikiuchisha Research Report Volume 23 Nankaido|publisher={{jalink|Kogakukan University|Q1797584}}Publishing Department|page=|isbn=|ref=}} ** {{Wikicite|reference=Suzuo Oku "Tomatsu Hime Shrine" |ref={{Harvid|Tomatsu Hime Shrine (Shikinai) | 1987}}}}, {{Wikicite|reference=Sadashi Nakamura "Takazumi Hiko Shrine/Takazumi Hime Shrine" |ref={{Harvid|Takazumi Hime Shrine/Takazumi Hime Shrine (Shikinai) | 1987}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=|title=Japanese Historical Place Name System 31 Place Names of Wakayama Prefecture|publisher={{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}|isbn=9784582490312|ref=}} ** {{Wikicite|reference="Kichire Village"|ref={{Harvid|Kichire Village (Taira)|1983}}}}, {{Wikicite|reference="Hirao Village"|ref={{Harvid|Hirao Village (Taira)|1983}}}}, {{Wikicite|reference="Takazumi Shrine"|ref={{Harvid|Takazumi Shrine (Taira)|1983}}}} === Related items === * {{jalink|Ooyatsuhime/Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047}} * Deities related to Kii capital Mato Hime no Mikoto ** {{jalink|Susa Shrine (Arita City)|Q11664501|Susa Shrine}} ({{jalink|Arita City|Q657843}} Senda, {{Wiki coordinates|34|3|53.55|N|135|8|29.32|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|location|name=Susa Shrine (Father God)}}) - Taisha|Q9610964}}) in {{jalink|Kiokuni|Q864053}}{{jalink|Nakusa District|Q11415547}}. === Overview === {{jalink|extension (Era name)|Q1327274|Extended}} "{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}" established in following three shrines in {{jalink|Wakayama Prefecture|Q131314}}{{jalink|Wakayama City|Q200747}} are said to be {{jalink|Ronsha|Q135022904}} in {{jalink|Shikiuchi}} ({{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964}}), which are described as ``Monthly New Year''. # '''{{jalink|#Miyakomatsuhime Shrine (Kichirei, Wakayama City)||Miyakomatsuhime Shrine}}''' (Kichirei, Wakayama City) - Former Old village company. → See {{jalink|Takazumi Shrine|Q17226307}}. === Enshrined God ===

As the company name suggests, it is said to be a shrine that enshrines '''{{jalink|Ooyatsuhime/Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|Tsumatsuhime}}''' (Tsumatsuhime no Mikoto, Sakutsuhime no Mikoto) as its enshrined deity.Regarding Tsumatsuhime, it is described in one book in "{{jalink|Nihon Shoki|Q813935}}" that she is the daughter god of {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoo}}. Also, the older brother god of Tsumatsuhime is {{jalink|Isotakejin|Q10881556|Isotakeru/Itakeru}}, and the older sister god is {{jalink|Ooyatsuhime/Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|Oyatsuhime no Mikoto}} ( Both gods are enshrined at {{jalink|Itaikiso Shrine|Q11379271}} (Itaikiso, Wakayama City) and {{jalink|Oyatsuhime Shrine|Q11434556}} (Udamori, Wakayama City), respectively. These three deities are said to be the gods who brought tree species to Kii Province, and in Kii they are collectively known as ``''Itaiki Sosanjin'' {{Sfn|Takazumi Shrine (Taira)|1983}}. === History === ==== Outline ==== The first reference to the historical material is the article in "{{jalink|Shoku Nihongi|Q611399}}" {{jalink|Taiho (Japan)|Q1346269|Taiho}} 2nd year ({{jalink|702|Q23497}})"Shoku Nihongi" article dated February 22, 702. In , there is an article about the separation of the three shrines of Ita-Kiso, Oya-Tohime, and Toma-Tohime.{{Sfn|Takazumi Shrine (Taira)|1983}}. "Shinsho Kaku Chofusho" {{jalink|Daido (Japan)|Q1373191|Daido}} 1st year ({{jalink|806|Q30021}})"Shinsho Kakuchofusho" 10 (Shinji Shoke Fudo) Daido 1st year (806). In , 13 houses were given to ``Tomazuhime no Kami'' of Kii Province {{Sfn|Takazumi Shrine (Taira)|1983}}. Related, {{jalink|Shohei (Japan)|Q1324874|Shohei}} ({{jalink|931|Q24789}}-{{jalink|938|Q24794}}) "{{jalink|Japanese name Ruijusho|Q2702 542}}', ``Tsuma (Tsuma) Go'' is seen in Kii Province {{jalink|Nakusa District|Q11415547}}, which is said to mean ``Tsuma Kobe'' {{Sfn|Takazumi Shrine (Taira)|1983}}.As {{jalink|Divine|Q11591025}}, {{jalink|Jōkan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jōkan}} In the first year of the year ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), he was promoted to the lower rank of Jushii (junior fourth rank) as ``Shinto Matsuhime'' along with Itagesojin and Ōyatsuhime no kami {{Sfn|Takazumi Shrine (Taira)|1983}}. {{jalink|Extension (era name)|Q1327274|Extension}} 5 years ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}) "{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}" {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|Shinmyocho}} states that in Nagusa District, Kii Province, there are "Tomatohime Shrine Meishindai It will be listed at {{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964}} as ``Monthly New Year'' and {{jalink|Monthly Festival|Q11516161}} and {{jalink| It is written that it was kept in {{jalink|heihaku|Q11481885}} at Niinamesai|Q11501518}}{{Sfn|Takazumi Shrine (Taira)|1983}}. In ``{{jalink|Kiokuni Shinmecho|Q17210008}}'', the Tenjin is listed as ``Junior Fourth Rank Upper Tumatsu Hime Okami'', and it is different from Itaukeso Okami, who rose to Shoichi Rank (Shoichi Rank), and has an open shrine {{Sfn|Takazumi Shrine (Taira)|1983}}. In addition, in the same book, there is also a related entry of ``Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Tsumutohime no Okami.'' Its subsequent history is unclear, and there are various theories as to which shrine the above shrine belongs to. ==== God level ==== * Records of {{jalink|Divine rank|Q11591025}} dedication in the history of the Six Kingdoms ** {{jalink|Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jogan}} On the 27th of the first month of the first year ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), the rank of Jufifth Rank, Lower Rank, Eighth Grade to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Rank, Eighth Grade (``{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}'') - The notation is ``Shinto Matsuhime God''. * After Six Kingdoms History ** Upper Juichii (``{{jalink|Kiokuni Shinto Name Book|Q17210008}}'') - Written as ``Tomato Hime Okami.'' Is it a typo in "Junior Fourth Rank Upper"? === Historical evidence === The exact location of Shikinai Shrine ``Tomatsu Hime Shrine'' is still uncertain. In ancient times, it is identified as Miyako Matsuhime Shrine (Kichirei, (1)) in Shandong in the "Kiokuni Famous Places Zue" and "Nanki Shrine Record" ({{jalink|Enkyo|Q1183728}}3 ({{jalink|1746|Q7589}})). Furthermore, in ``{{jalink|Kii Zoku Fudoki|Q11604964}}'', it is said that Matsuhime Shrine (1), the capital of Shandong, which is mentioned in ``Nanki Shrine Roku'', was originally dedicated to ``Yoshire Tsuhime no Mikoto'' {{Sfn|Takazumi Shrine (Taira)|1983}}. Yoshiretsuhime-no-Mikoto appears in the ``Kiokuni Jinmyocho'' as ``Junior Fifth Rank Lower, Yoshiretsu Hime-no-Kami.'' In addition, in "Zoku Fudoki", there is a theory that Hirao's wife Daimyo Shrine (currently Tomatohime Shrine, (2)) is referred to as ``Toumatsuhime Shrine'' {{Sfn|Takazumi Shrine (Taira)|1983}}. One of these theories is that {{jalink|Water theory|Q2464727}} ({{jalink|Water theory of Szene-Kunikake Shrine and Kodaimyojin|Q11504966}}) that occurred between {{jalink|Maezen Shrine/Kokukake Shrine|Q888184}} and Wasaso. |Q1193059}}5 ({{jalink|1433|Q6356}}) states that Takazumi Shrine (3) was originally located in Maemiya, and later moved its enshrined place to Shandong, and later to Mt. Wasa.{{Sfn|Takazumi Shrine (Taira)|1983}}. Since this legend is consistent with an article written in 702 in the ``Shoku Nihongi'', ``Zoku Fudoki'' states that there is a high possibility that Takazumi Shrine is Tomatohime Shrine {{Sfn|Takazumi Shrine (Taira)|1983}}. === Miyakomatsuhime Shrine (Yoshire, Wakayama City) === {{Shrine |Name = Miyakomatsuhime Shrine |Image = [[File:Tsumatsuhime-jinja (Kire) shaden.JPG|280px]]

Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|34|12|18.49|N|135|14|6.79|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|12|18.49|N|135|14|6.79|E}}}} }} '''Tsumatsuhime Shrine'' (Tsumatsuhime Shrine) is located in {{jalink|Wakayama Prefecture|Q131314}}{{jalink|Wakayama City|Q200747}}Yoshire. {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964}}) {{jalink|Ronsha|Q135022904}}, {{jalink|Modern shrine rank system|Q712534|Old shrine rank}} is {{jalink|Murasha|Q134917284}}. === Enshrined God === '''Main enshrined deity''' * {{jalink|Ooyatsuhime・Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|Miyakomatsuhime no Mikoto}} * Aiden: Yoshiretsuhime no Mikoto '''Deity enshrined'' * Left Hall: {{jalink|Goju Takekami|Q10881556|Goju Takekami}} * Right shrine: {{jalink|Ooyatsuhime Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|Oyatsuhime no Mikoto}} === History === {{jalink|Kiokuni|Q864053}}{{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964}} "Tomatsu Hime Shrine" in {{jalink|Nakusa District|Q11415547}} {{jalink|Ronsha|Q135022904}}. This is compared to our company in "Ki Province Meisho Zue" and "Nanki Shrine Record" ({{jalink|Enkyo|Q1183728}}3 ({{jalink|1746|Q7589}})). On the other hand, "Zoku Fudoki" denies this, and our company says that it is a shrine that enshrines the "Junior Fifth Rank Lower Yoshiretsuhime-no-kami" that appears in the "Kiokuni Jinmyocho", and compares Matsumatsuhime-kami to Takazumi Shrine. According to the shrine statement book, our company was dedicated as Shikinai Shrine ``Miyatomatsuhime Shrine'' during the Edo period, but in {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} it was renamed to ``Yoshiretsuhime Shrine'' {{Sfn|Miyatomatsu Hime Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987|p=51-52}}. In the 6th year of the Meiji era ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}), it was ranked as {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} in the {{jalink|modern shrine rank system|Q712534|old shrine rank}}. After that, in {{jalink|Showa|Q327567}}21 ({{jalink|1946|Q18610}}), it reverted to its former name ``Miyakomatsuhime Shrine'' and remains the current name. === Setsusha === * The description of Inari Shrine Setsusue Shrine is from [http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=1053 Miyakomatsuhime Shrine (Kichire)] (Wakayama Prefectural Shrine Agency). * Benzaitensha * Gionsha * Goshusha * Kawagozensha * Goryosha * Sofusha File:Tsumatsuhime-jinja (Kire) keidai.JPG|Precincts File:Tsumatsuhime-jinja (Kire) torii.JPG|Torii === Miyamato Hime Shrine (Hirao, Wakayama City) === {{Shrine |Name = Tomatsu Hime Shrine |Image = [[File:Tsumatsuhime-jinja (Hirao) shaden.JPG|280px]]

Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|34|12|30.30|N|135|14|49.90|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|12|30.30|N|135|14|49.90|E}}}} }} '''Tsumatsu Hime Shrine'' (Tsumatsu Hime Jinja, Miyako Mazu Hime Shrine) is located in {{jalink|Wakayama Prefecture|Q131314}}{{jalink|Wakayama City|Q200747}}Hirao. {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964}}) {{jalink|Ronsha|Q135022904}}. {{Sfn|Tomatsu Hime Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine) |1987|p=51-52}} adjacent to the ruins of Heioji Shrine on the Kumano Kodo. === Enshrined God === * {{jalink|Ooyatsuhime・Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|Miyakomatsuhime no Mikoto}} === History === {{jalink|Kiokuni|Q864053}}{{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964}} "Tomatsu Hime Shrine" in {{jalink|Nakusa District|Q11415547}} {{jalink|Ronsha|Q135022904}}. In ``Zoku Fudoki'', there is a theory that compares the company to this, and in addition, there is a theory that compares it to ``Junior Fourth Rank Lower, Tsumatsuhiba Okami'' in the ``Kiokuni Jinmyocho'' {{Sfn|Hirao Village (Taira)|1983}}. The company name is ``Tsumadaimyo Shrine'' in ``Zoku Fudoki,'' and ``Tsuma Gozen Shrine'' in ``Kiokuni Meisho Zue.'' According to "Zoku Fudoki", the precincts were once two and a half towns and the shrine building was majestic, but it fell into disrepair during the Tensho War, and although it was revived by {{jalink|Toyotomi Hidenaga|Q1191183}}, it declined. Currently, a small shrine is enshrined in the precincts of about 6 steps. Two stone lanterns dating back to {{jalink|Original|Q1145754}}5 ({{jalink|1740|Q7006}}) stand in the shrine grounds. Furthermore, according to "Kanei-ki", it is said that on the first day of the New Year, the first day of the Pig in October, and the first day of the New Year in November, members of the shrine of Ita-keiso Shrine once served the company. === Takazumi Shrine === {{See|Takazumi Shrine}} === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} '''Notes''' {{reflist|group="Note"}} '''Original source''': Introducing the primary historical sources for the items mentioned. {{reflist|group="hara"}} '''Source''' {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor=Edited by Shikinaisha Research Group|author=|chapter=|year=1987|title=Shikiuchisha Research Report Volume 23 Nankaido|publisher={{jalink|Kogakukan University|Q1797584}}Publishing Department|page=|isbn=|ref=}} ** {{Wikicite|reference=Suzuo Oku "Tomatsu Hime Shrine" |ref={{Harvid|Tomatsu Hime Shrine (Shikinai) | 1987}}}}, {{Wikicite|reference=Sadashi Nakamura "Takazumi Hiko Shrine/Takazumi Hime Shrine" |ref={{Harvid|Takazumi Hime Shrine/Takazumi Hime Shrine (Shikinai) | 1987}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=|title=Japanese Historical Place Name System 31 Place Names of Wakayama Prefecture|publisher={{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}|isbn=9784582490312|ref=}} ** {{Wikicite|reference="Kichire Village"|ref={{Harvid|Kichire Village (Taira)|1983}}}}, {{Wikicite|reference="Hirao Village"|ref={{Harvid|Hirao Village (Taira)|1983}}}}, {{Wikicite|reference="Takazumi Shrine"|ref={{Harvid|Takazumi Shrine (Taira)|1983}}}} === Related items === * {{jalink|Ooyatsuhime/Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047}} * Deities related to Kii capital Mato Hime no Mikoto ** {{jalink|Susa Shrine (Arita City)|Q11664501|Susa Shrine}} ({{jalink|Arita City|Q657843}} Senda, {{Wiki coordinates|34|3|53.55|N|135|8|29.32|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|location|name=Susa Shrine (Father God)}}) - As the company name suggests, it is said to be a shrine that enshrines '''{{jalink|Ooyatsuhime/Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|Tsumatsuhime}}''' (Tsumatsuhime no Mikoto, Sakutsuhime no Mikoto) as its enshrined deity.Regarding Tsumatsuhime, it is described in one book in "{{jalink|Nihon Shoki|Q813935}}" that she is the daughter god of {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoo}}. Also, the older brother god of Tsumatsuhime is {{jalink|Isotakejin|Q10881556|Isotakeru/Itakeru}}, and the older sister god is {{jalink|Ooyatsuhime/Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|Oyatsuhime no Mikoto}} ( Both gods are enshrined at {{jalink|Itaikiso Shrine|Q11379271}} (Itaikiso, Wakayama City) and {{jalink|Oyatsuhime Shrine|Q11434556}} (Udamori, Wakayama City), respectively. These three deities are said to be the gods who brought tree species to Kii Province, and in Kii they are collectively known as ``''Itaiki Sosanjin'' {{Sfn|Takazumi Shrine (Taira)|1983}}. === History === ==== Outline ==== The first reference to the historical material is the article in "{{jalink|Shoku Nihongi|Q611399}}" {{jalink|Taiho (Japan)|Q1346269|Taiho}} 2nd year ({{jalink|702|Q23497}})"Shoku Nihongi" article dated February 22, 702. In , there is an article about the separation of the three shrines of Ita-Kiso, Oya-Tohime, and Toma-Tohime.{{Sfn|Takazumi Shrine (Taira)|1983}}. "Shinsho Kaku Chofusho" {{jalink|Daido (Japan)|Q1373191|Daido}} 1st year ({{jalink|806|Q30021}})"Shinsho Kakuchofusho" 10 (Shinji Shoke Fudo) Daido 1st year (806). In , 13 houses were given to ``Tomazuhime no Kami'' of Kii Province {{Sfn|Takazumi Shrine (Taira)|1983}}. Related, {{jalink|Shohei (Japan)|Q1324874|Shohei}} ({{jalink|931|Q24789}}-{{jalink|938|Q24794}}) "{{jalink|Japanese name Ruijusho|Q2702 542}}', ``Tsuma (Tsuma) Go'' is seen in Kii Province {{jalink|Nakusa District|Q11415547}}, which is said to mean ``Tsuma Kobe'' {{Sfn|Takazumi Shrine (Taira)|1983}}.As {{jalink|Divine|Q11591025}}, {{jalink|Jōkan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jōkan}} In the first year of the year ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), he was promoted to the lower rank of Jushii (junior fourth rank) as ``Shinto Matsuhime'' along with Itagesojin and Ōyatsuhime no kami {{Sfn|Takazumi Shrine (Taira)|1983}}. {{jalink|Extension (era name)|Q1327274|Extension}} 5 years ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}) "{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}" {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|Shinmyocho}} states that in Nagusa District, Kii Province, there are "Tomatohime Shrine Meishindai It will be listed at {{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964}} as ``Monthly New Year'' and {{jalink|Monthly Festival|Q11516161}} and {{jalink| It is written that it was kept in {{jalink|heihaku|Q11481885}} at Niinamesai|Q11501518}}{{Sfn|Takazumi Shrine (Taira)|1983}}. In ``{{jalink|Kiokuni Shinmecho|Q17210008}}'', the Tenjin is listed as ``Junior Fourth Rank Upper Tumatsu Hime Okami'', and it is different from Itaukeso Okami, who rose to Shoichi Rank (Shoichi Rank), and has an open shrine {{Sfn|Takazumi Shrine (Taira)|1983}}. In addition, in the same book, there is also a related entry of ``Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Tsumutohime no Okami.'' Its subsequent history is unclear, and there are various theories as to which shrine the above shrine belongs to. ==== God level ==== * Records of {{jalink|Divine rank|Q11591025}} dedication in the history of the Six Kingdoms ** {{jalink|Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jogan}} On the 27th of the first month of the first year ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), the rank of Jufifth Rank, Lower Rank, Eighth Grade to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Rank, Eighth Grade (``{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}'') - The notation is ``Shinto Matsuhime God''. * After Six Kingdoms History ** Upper Juichii (``{{jalink|Kiokuni Shinto Name Book|Q17210008}}'') - Written as ``Tomato Hime Okami.'' Is it a typo in "Junior Fourth Rank Upper"? === Historical evidence === The exact location of Shikinai Shrine ``Tomatsu Hime Shrine'' is still uncertain. In ancient times, it is identified as Miyako Matsuhime Shrine (Kichirei, (1)) in Shandong in the "Kiokuni Famous Places Zue" and "Nanki Shrine Record" ({{jalink|Enkyo|Q1183728}}3 ({{jalink|1746|Q7589}})). Furthermore, in ``{{jalink|Kii Zoku Fudoki|Q11604964}}'', it is said that Matsuhime Shrine (1), the capital of Shandong, which is mentioned in ``Nanki Shrine Roku'', was originally dedicated to ``Yoshire Tsuhime no Mikoto'' {{Sfn|Takazumi Shrine (Taira)|1983}}. Yoshiretsuhime-no-Mikoto appears in the ``Kiokuni Jinmyocho'' as ``Junior Fifth Rank Lower, Yoshiretsu Hime-no-Kami.'' In addition, in "Zoku Fudoki", there is a theory that Hirao's wife Daimyo Shrine (currently Tomatohime Shrine, (2)) is referred to as ``Toumatsuhime Shrine'' {{Sfn|Takazumi Shrine (Taira)|1983}}. One of these theories is that {{jalink|Water theory|Q2464727}} ({{jalink|Water theory of Szene-Kunikake Shrine and Kodaimyojin|Q11504966}}) that occurred between {{jalink|Maezen Shrine/Kokukake Shrine|Q888184}} and Wasaso. |Q1193059}}5 ({{jalink|1433|Q6356}}) states that Takazumi Shrine (3) was originally located in Maemiya, and later moved its enshrined place to Shandong, and later to Mt. Wasa.{{Sfn|Takazumi Shrine (Taira)|1983}}. Since this legend is consistent with an article written in 702 in the ``Shoku Nihongi'', ``Zoku Fudoki'' states that there is a high possibility that Takazumi Shrine is Tomatohime Shrine {{Sfn|Takazumi Shrine (Taira)|1983}}. === Miyakomatsuhime Shrine (Yoshire, Wakayama City) === {{Shrine |Name = Miyakomatsuhime Shrine |Image = [[File:Tsumatsuhime-jinja (Kire) shaden.JPG|280px]]
Shrine building |Location = {{jalink|Wakayama Prefecture|Q131314}}{{jalink|Wakayama City|Q200747}}Yoshire 911 |Position = {{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|34|12|18.49|N|135|14|6.79|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|name=Miyakomatsuhime Shrine (Kichire)}} |Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Ooyatsuhime/Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|Miyakomatsuhime no Mikoto}} |Divinity = |Shakaku = {{jalink|Shikiuchisha}} ({{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|Meishindai}}) {{jalink|Ronsha|Q135022904}}

Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|34|12|18.49|N|135|14|6.79|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|12|18.49|N|135|14|6.79|E}}}} }} '''Tsumatsuhime Shrine'' (Tsumatsuhime Shrine) is located in {{jalink|Wakayama Prefecture|Q131314}}{{jalink|Wakayama City|Q200747}}Yoshire. {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964}}) {{jalink|Ronsha|Q135022904}}, {{jalink|Modern shrine rank system|Q712534|Old shrine rank}} is {{jalink|Murasha|Q134917284}}. === Enshrined God === '''Main enshrined deity''' * {{jalink|Ooyatsuhime・Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|Miyakomatsuhime no Mikoto}} * Aiden: Yoshiretsuhime no Mikoto '''Deity enshrined'' * Left Hall: {{jalink|Goju Takekami|Q10881556|Goju Takekami}} * Right shrine: {{jalink|Ooyatsuhime Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|Oyatsuhime no Mikoto}} === History === {{jalink|Kiokuni|Q864053}}{{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964}} "Tomatsu Hime Shrine" in {{jalink|Nakusa District|Q11415547}} {{jalink|Ronsha|Q135022904}}. This is compared to our company in "Ki Province Meisho Zue" and "Nanki Shrine Record" ({{jalink|Enkyo|Q1183728}}3 ({{jalink|1746|Q7589}})). On the other hand, "Zoku Fudoki" denies this, and our company says that it is a shrine that enshrines the "Junior Fifth Rank Lower Yoshiretsuhime-no-kami" that appears in the "Kiokuni Jinmyocho", and compares Matsumatsuhime-kami to Takazumi Shrine. According to the shrine statement book, our company was dedicated as Shikinai Shrine ``Miyatomatsuhime Shrine'' during the Edo period, but in {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} it was renamed to ``Yoshiretsuhime Shrine'' {{Sfn|Miyatomatsu Hime Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987|p=51-52}}. In the 6th year of the Meiji era ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}), it was ranked as {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} in the {{jalink|modern shrine rank system|Q712534|old shrine rank}}. After that, in {{jalink|Showa|Q327567}}21 ({{jalink|1946|Q18610}}), it reverted to its former name ``Miyakomatsuhime Shrine'' and remains the current name. === Setsusha === * The description of Inari Shrine Setsusue Shrine is from [http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=1053 Miyakomatsuhime Shrine (Kichire)] (Wakayama Prefectural Shrine Agency). * Benzaitensha * Gionsha * Goshusha * Kawagozensha * Goryosha * Sofusha File:Tsumatsuhime-jinja (Kire) keidai.JPG|Precincts File:Tsumatsuhime-jinja (Kire) torii.JPG|Torii === Miyamato Hime Shrine (Hirao, Wakayama City) === {{Shrine |Name = Tomatsu Hime Shrine |Image = [[File:Tsumatsuhime-jinja (Hirao) shaden.JPG|280px]]
Shrine building |Location = {{jalink|Wakayama Prefecture|Q131314}}{{jalink|Wakayama City|Q200747}}Hirao Aza Wakabayashi 957 |Position = {{Wiki coordinates 2 degrees, minutes, seconds|34|12|30.30|N|135|14|49.90|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|name=Tomato Hime Shrine (Hirao)}} |Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Ooyatsuhime/Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|Miyakomatsuhime no Mikoto}} |Divinity = |Shakaku = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|Meishin University}}) {{jalink|Ronsha|Q135022904}} |Foundation = unknown |Main shrine = {{jalink|Ryuzo|Q210684}} |alias= |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular Festival = First Pig Day in October {{Sfn|Tomato Hime Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987|p=51-52}} |Shinto ritual = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Wakayama|label=Miyakomatsuhime
Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|34|12|30.30|N|135|14|49.90|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|12|30.30|N|135|14|49.90|E}}}} }} '''Tsumatsu Hime Shrine'' (Tsumatsu Hime Jinja, Miyako Mazu Hime Shrine) is located in {{jalink|Wakayama Prefecture|Q131314}}{{jalink|Wakayama City|Q200747}}Hirao. {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964}}) {{jalink|Ronsha|Q135022904}}. {{Sfn|Tomatsu Hime Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine) |1987|p=51-52}} adjacent to the ruins of Heioji Shrine on the Kumano Kodo. === Enshrined God === * {{jalink|Ooyatsuhime・Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|Miyakomatsuhime no Mikoto}} === History === {{jalink|Kiokuni|Q864053}}{{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964}} "Tomatsu Hime Shrine" in {{jalink|Nakusa District|Q11415547}} {{jalink|Ronsha|Q135022904}}. In ``Zoku Fudoki'', there is a theory that compares the company to this, and in addition, there is a theory that compares it to ``Junior Fourth Rank Lower, Tsumatsuhiba Okami'' in the ``Kiokuni Jinmyocho'' {{Sfn|Hirao Village (Taira)|1983}}. The company name is ``Tsumadaimyo Shrine'' in ``Zoku Fudoki,'' and ``Tsuma Gozen Shrine'' in ``Kiokuni Meisho Zue.'' According to "Zoku Fudoki", the precincts were once two and a half towns and the shrine building was majestic, but it fell into disrepair during the Tensho War, and although it was revived by {{jalink|Toyotomi Hidenaga|Q1191183}}, it declined. Currently, a small shrine is enshrined in the precincts of about 6 steps. Two stone lanterns dating back to {{jalink|Original|Q1145754}}5 ({{jalink|1740|Q7006}}) stand in the shrine grounds. Furthermore, according to "Kanei-ki", it is said that on the first day of the New Year, the first day of the Pig in October, and the first day of the New Year in November, members of the shrine of Ita-keiso Shrine once served the company. === Takazumi Shrine === {{See|Takazumi Shrine}} === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} '''Notes''' {{reflist|group="Note"}} '''Original source''': Introducing the primary historical sources for the items mentioned. {{reflist|group="hara"}} '''Source''' {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor=Edited by Shikinaisha Research Group|author=|chapter=|year=1987|title=Shikiuchisha Research Report Volume 23 Nankaido|publisher={{jalink|Kogakukan University|Q1797584}}Publishing Department|page=|isbn=|ref=}} ** {{Wikicite|reference=Suzuo Oku "Tomatsu Hime Shrine" |ref={{Harvid|Tomatsu Hime Shrine (Shikinai) | 1987}}}}, {{Wikicite|reference=Sadashi Nakamura "Takazumi Hiko Shrine/Takazumi Hime Shrine" |ref={{Harvid|Takazumi Hime Shrine/Takazumi Hime Shrine (Shikinai) | 1987}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=|title=Japanese Historical Place Name System 31 Place Names of Wakayama Prefecture|publisher={{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}|isbn=9784582490312|ref=}} ** {{Wikicite|reference="Kichire Village"|ref={{Harvid|Kichire Village (Taira)|1983}}}}, {{Wikicite|reference="Hirao Village"|ref={{Harvid|Hirao Village (Taira)|1983}}}}, {{Wikicite|reference="Takazumi Shrine"|ref={{Harvid|Takazumi Shrine (Taira)|1983}}}} === Related items === * {{jalink|Ooyatsuhime/Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047}} * Deities related to Kii capital Mato Hime no Mikoto ** {{jalink|Susa Shrine (Arita City)|Q11664501|Susa Shrine}} ({{jalink|Arita City|Q657843}} Senda, {{Wiki coordinates|34|3|53.55|N|135|8|29.32|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|location|name=Susa Shrine (Father God)}}) - ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}), which are described as ``Monthly New Year''. # '''{{ill|lt=#Miyakomatsuhime Shrine (Kichirei, Wakayama City)||WD=|lt=Miyakomatsuhime Shrine}}''' (Kichirei, Wakayama City) - Former {{ill|lt=Murasha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. # '''{{ill|lt=#Tomatohime Shrine (Hirao, Wakayama City)||WD=|lt=Tomatohime Shrine}}''' (Hirao, Wakayama City) # Shrine}}) - It is also {{ill|lt=Ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}} of Shikinaisha ``Takazumi Hime Shrine'' and ``Takazumi Hime Shrine.'' Old village company. → See {{ill|lt=Takazumi Shrine|Q17226307|WD=Q17226307}}. === Enshrined God ===
'''Abbreviated genealogy of the three Ita-kei gods'' (written according to the Nihon Shoki) {{familytree/start|style=font-size:100%}} {{familytree|border=0||||01| 01={{ill|lt=Susano-o|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susa no Takashi}}
({{ill|lt=Susa Shrine (Arita City)|Q11664501|WD=Q11664501|lt=Susa Shrine}})}} {{familytree|border=0|||,|-|+|-|-|.| }}{{familytree|border=0|01|02|03| 01={{ill|lt=50 Fierce God|Q10881556|WD=Q10881556|lt=50 Fierce Life}}
({{ill|lt=Itaukeso Shrine|Q11379271|WD=Q11379271}})|02={{jalink|Ooyatsuhime Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|{{color|#FC4E6B|Oyatsuhime no Mikoto}}}}
({{ill|lt=Oyatsuhime Shrine|Q11434556|WD=Q11434556|lt=Oyatsuhime Shrine}})|03='''{{jalink|Ooyatsuhime Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|{{color|#FC4E6B|Hamatsuhime no Mikoto}}}}'''
('''Miyatsuhime Shrine''')}} {{familytree/end}}
As the company name suggests, it is said to be a shrine that enshrines '''{{ill|lt=Ooyatsuhime/Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|WD=Q10935047|lt=Tsumatsuhime}}''' (Tsumatsuhime no Mikoto, Sakutsuhime no Mikoto) as its enshrined deity.Regarding Tsumatsuhime, it is described in one book in "{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935}}" that she is the daughter god of {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo}}. Also, the older brother god of Tsumatsuhime is {{ill|lt=Isotakejin|Q10881556|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru/Itakeru}}, and the older sister god is {{ill|lt=Ooyatsuhime/Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|WD=Q10935047|lt=Oyatsuhime no Mikoto}} ( Both gods are enshrined at {{ill|lt=Itaikiso Shrine|Q11379271|WD=Q11379271}} (Itaikiso, Wakayama City) and {{ill|lt=Oyatsuhime Shrine|Q11434556|WD=Q11434556}} (Udamori, Wakayama City), respectively. These three deities are said to be the gods who brought tree species to Kii Province, and in Kii they are collectively known as ``''Itaiki Sosanjin'' {{Sfn|Takazumi Shrine (Taira)|1983}}. === History === ==== Outline ==== The first reference to the historical material is the article in "{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399}}" {{ill|lt=Taiho (Japan)|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taiho}} 2nd year ({{ill|lt=702|Q23497|WD=Q23497}})"Shoku Nihongi" article dated February 22, 702. In , there is an article about the separation of the three shrines of Ita-Kiso, Oya-Tohime, and Toma-Tohime.{{Sfn|Takazumi Shrine (Taira)|1983}}. "Shinsho Kaku Chofusho" {{ill|lt=Daido (Japan)|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daido}} 1st year ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021}})"Shinsho Kakuchofusho" 10 (Shinji Shoke Fudo) Daido 1st year (806). In , 13 houses were given to ``Tomazuhime no Kami'' of Kii Province {{Sfn|Takazumi Shrine (Taira)|1983}}. Related, {{ill|lt=Shohei (Japan)|Q1324874|WD=Q1324874|lt=Shohei}} ({{ill|lt=931|Q24789|WD=Q24789}}-{{ill|lt=938|Q24794|WD=Q24794}}) "{{ill||lt=Japanese name Ruijusho|UNKNOWN}}', ``Tsuma (Tsuma) Go'' is seen in Kii Province {{ill|lt=Nakusa District|Q11415547|WD=Q11415547}}, which is said to mean ``Tsuma Kobe'' {{Sfn|Takazumi Shrine (Taira)|1983}}.As {{ill|lt=Divine|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025}}, {{ill|lt=Jōkan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōkan}} In the first year of the year ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897}}), he was promoted to the lower rank of Jushii (junior fourth rank) as ``Shinto Matsuhime'' along with Itagesojin and Ōyatsuhime no kami {{Sfn|Takazumi Shrine (Taira)|1983}}. {{ill|lt=Extension (era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Extension}} 5 years ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}) "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}" {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shinmyocho}} states that in Nagusa District, Kii Province, there are "Tomatohime Shrine Meishindai It will be listed at {{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}} as ``Monthly New Year'' and {{ill|lt=Monthly Festival|Q11516161|WD=Q11516161}} and {{jalink| It is written that it was kept in {{ill|lt=heihaku|Q11481885|WD=Q11481885}} at Niinamesai|Q11501518}}{{Sfn|Takazumi Shrine (Taira)|1983}}. In ``{{ill|lt=Kiokuni Shinmecho|Q17210008|WD=Q17210008}}'', the Tenjin is listed as ``Junior Fourth Rank Upper Tumatsu Hime Okami'', and it is different from Itaukeso Okami, who rose to Shoichi Rank (Shoichi Rank), and has an open shrine {{Sfn|Takazumi Shrine (Taira)|1983}}. In addition, in the same book, there is also a related entry of ``Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Tsumutohime no Okami.'' Its subsequent history is unclear, and there are various theories as to which shrine the above shrine belongs to. ==== God level ==== * Records of {{ill|lt=Divine rank|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025}} dedication in the history of the Six Kingdoms ** {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan}} On the 27th of the first month of the first year ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897}}), the rank of Jufifth Rank, Lower Rank, Eighth Grade to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Rank, Eighth Grade (``{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}'') - The notation is ``Shinto Matsuhime God''. * After Six Kingdoms History ** Upper Juichii (``{{ill|lt=Kiokuni Shinto Name Book|Q17210008|WD=Q17210008}}'') - Written as ``Tomato Hime Okami.'' Is it a typo in "Junior Fourth Rank Upper"? === Historical evidence === The exact location of Shikinai Shrine ``Tomatsu Hime Shrine'' is still uncertain. In ancient times, it is identified as Miyako Matsuhime Shrine (Kichirei, (1)) in Shandong in the "Kiokuni Famous Places Zue" and "Nanki Shrine Record" ({{ill|lt=Enkyo|Q1183728|WD=Q1183728}}3 ({{ill|lt=1746|Q7589|WD=Q7589}})). Furthermore, in ``{{ill|lt=Kii Zoku Fudoki|Q11604964|WD=Q11604964}}'', it is said that Matsuhime Shrine (1), the capital of Shandong, which is mentioned in ``Nanki Shrine Roku'', was originally dedicated to ``Yoshire Tsuhime no Mikoto'' {{Sfn|Takazumi Shrine (Taira)|1983}}. Yoshiretsuhime-no-Mikoto appears in the ``Kiokuni Jinmyocho'' as ``Junior Fifth Rank Lower, Yoshiretsu Hime-no-Kami.'' In addition, in "Zoku Fudoki", there is a theory that Hirao's wife Daimyo Shrine (currently Tomatohime Shrine, (2)) is referred to as ``Toumatsuhime Shrine'' {{Sfn|Takazumi Shrine (Taira)|1983}}. One of these theories is that {{ill|lt=Water theory|Q2464727|WD=Q2464727}} ({{ill|lt=Water theory of Szene-Kunikake Shrine and Kodaimyojin|Q11504966|WD=Q11504966}}) that occurred between {{ill|lt=Maezen Shrine/Kokukake Shrine|Q888184|WD=Q888184}} and Wasaso. |Q1193059}}5 ({{ill|lt=1433|Q6356|WD=Q6356}}) states that Takazumi Shrine (3) was originally located in Maemiya, and later moved its enshrined place to Shandong, and later to Mt. Wasa.{{Sfn|Takazumi Shrine (Taira)|1983}}. Since this legend is consistent with an article written in 702 in the ``Shoku Nihongi'', ``Zoku Fudoki'' states that there is a high possibility that Takazumi Shrine is Tomatohime Shrine {{Sfn|Takazumi Shrine (Taira)|1983}}. === Miyakomatsuhime Shrine (Yoshire, Wakayama City) === {{Shrine |Name = Miyakomatsuhime Shrine |Image = [[File:Tsumatsuhime-jinja (Kire) shaden.JPG|280px]]

Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|34|12|18.49|N|135|14|6.79|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|12|18.49|N|135|14|6.79|E}}}} }} '''Tsumatsuhime Shrine'' (Tsumatsuhime Shrine) is located in {{ill|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|Q131314|WD=Q131314}}{{ill|lt=Wakayama City|Q200747|WD=Q200747}}Yoshire. {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}) {{ill|lt=Ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}}, {{ill|lt=Modern shrine rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old shrine rank}} is {{ill|lt=Murasha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. === Enshrined God === '''Main enshrined deity''' * {{ill|lt=Ooyatsuhime・Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|WD=Q10935047|lt=Miyakomatsuhime no Mikoto}} * Aiden: Yoshiretsuhime no Mikoto '''Deity enshrined'' * Left Hall: {{ill|lt=Goju Takekami|Q10881556|WD=Q10881556|lt=Goju Takekami}} * Right shrine: {{ill|lt=Ooyatsuhime Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|WD=Q10935047|lt=Oyatsuhime no Mikoto}} === History === {{ill|lt=Kiokuni|Q864053|WD=Q864053}}{{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}} "Tomatsu Hime Shrine" in {{ill|lt=Nakusa District|Q11415547|WD=Q11415547}} {{ill|lt=Ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}}. This is compared to our company in "Ki Province Meisho Zue" and "Nanki Shrine Record" ({{ill|lt=Enkyo|Q1183728|WD=Q1183728}}3 ({{ill|lt=1746|Q7589|WD=Q7589}})). On the other hand, "Zoku Fudoki" denies this, and our company says that it is a shrine that enshrines the "Junior Fifth Rank Lower Yoshiretsuhime-no-kami" that appears in the "Kiokuni Jinmyocho", and compares Matsumatsuhime-kami to Takazumi Shrine. According to the shrine statement book, our company was dedicated as Shikinai Shrine ``Miyatomatsuhime Shrine'' during the Edo period, but in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} it was renamed to ``Yoshiretsuhime Shrine'' {{Sfn|Miyatomatsu Hime Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987|p=51-52}}. In the 6th year of the Meiji era ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}), it was ranked as {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} in the {{ill|lt=modern shrine rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=old shrine rank}}. After that, in {{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}}21 ({{ill|lt=1946|Q18610|WD=Q18610}}), it reverted to its former name ``Miyakomatsuhime Shrine'' and remains the current name. === Setsusha === * The description of Inari Shrine Setsusue Shrine is from [http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=1053 Miyakomatsuhime Shrine (Kichire)] (Wakayama Prefectural Shrine Agency). * Benzaitensha * Gionsha * Goshusha * Kawagozensha * Goryosha * Sofusha File:Tsumatsuhime-jinja (Kire) keidai.JPG|Precincts File:Tsumatsuhime-jinja (Kire) torii.JPG|Torii === Miyamato Hime Shrine (Hirao, Wakayama City) === {{Shrine |Name = Tomatsu Hime Shrine |Image = [[File:Tsumatsuhime-jinja (Hirao) shaden.JPG|280px]]

Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|34|12|30.30|N|135|14|49.90|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|12|30.30|N|135|14|49.90|E}}}} }} '''Tsumatsu Hime Shrine'' (Tsumatsu Hime Jinja, Miyako Mazu Hime Shrine) is located in {{ill|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|Q131314|WD=Q131314}}{{ill|lt=Wakayama City|Q200747|WD=Q200747}}Hirao. {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}) {{ill|lt=Ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}}. {{Sfn|Tomatsu Hime Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine) |1987|p=51-52}} adjacent to the ruins of Heioji Shrine on the Kumano Kodo. === Enshrined God === * {{ill|lt=Ooyatsuhime・Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|WD=Q10935047|lt=Miyakomatsuhime no Mikoto}} === History === {{ill|lt=Kiokuni|Q864053|WD=Q864053}}{{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}} "Tomatsu Hime Shrine" in {{ill|lt=Nakusa District|Q11415547|WD=Q11415547}} {{ill|lt=Ronsha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}}. In ``Zoku Fudoki'', there is a theory that compares the company to this, and in addition, there is a theory that compares it to ``Junior Fourth Rank Lower, Tsumatsuhiba Okami'' in the ``Kiokuni Jinmyocho'' {{Sfn|Hirao Village (Taira)|1983}}. The company name is ``Tsumadaimyo Shrine'' in ``Zoku Fudoki,'' and ``Tsuma Gozen Shrine'' in ``Kiokuni Meisho Zue.'' According to "Zoku Fudoki", the precincts were once two and a half towns and the shrine building was majestic, but it fell into disrepair during the Tensho War, and although it was revived by {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hidenaga|Q1191183|WD=Q1191183}}, it declined. Currently, a small shrine is enshrined in the precincts of about 6 steps. Two stone lanterns dating back to {{ill|lt=Original|Q1145754|WD=Q1145754}}5 ({{ill|lt=1740|Q7006|WD=Q7006}}) stand in the shrine grounds. Furthermore, according to "Kanei-ki", it is said that on the first day of the New Year, the first day of the Pig in October, and the first day of the New Year in November, members of the shrine of Ita-keiso Shrine once served the company. === Takazumi Shrine === {{See|Takazumi Shrine}} === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} '''Notes''' {{reflist|group="Note"}} '''Original source''': Introducing the primary historical sources for the items mentioned. {{reflist|group="hara"}} '''Source''' {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor=Edited by Shikinaisha Research Group|author=|chapter=|year=1987|title=Shikiuchisha Research Report Volume 23 Nankaido|publisher={{ill|lt=Kogakukan University|Q1797584|WD=Q1797584}}Publishing Department|page=|isbn=|ref=}} ** {{Wikicite|reference=Suzuo Oku "Tomatsu Hime Shrine" |ref={{Harvid|Tomatsu Hime Shrine (Shikinai) | 1987}}}}, {{Wikicite|reference=Sadashi Nakamura "Takazumi Hiko Shrine/Takazumi Hime Shrine" |ref={{Harvid|Takazumi Hime Shrine/Takazumi Hime Shrine (Shikinai) | 1987}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=|title=Japanese Historical Place Name System 31 Place Names of Wakayama Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}|isbn=9784582490312|ref=}} ** {{Wikicite|reference="Kichire Village"|ref={{Harvid|Kichire Village (Taira)|1983}}}}, {{Wikicite|reference="Hirao Village"|ref={{Harvid|Hirao Village (Taira)|1983}}}}, {{Wikicite|reference="Takazumi Shrine"|ref={{Harvid|Takazumi Shrine (Taira)|1983}}}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Ooyatsuhime/Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|WD=Q10935047}} * Deities related to Kii capital Mato Hime no Mikoto ** {{ill|lt=Susa Shrine (Arita City)|Q11664501|WD=Q11664501|lt=Susa Shrine}} ({{ill|lt=Arita City|Q657843|WD=Q657843}} Senda, {{Wiki coordinates|34|3|53.55|N|135|8|29.32|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|location|name=Susa Shrine (Father God)}}) - '''Abbreviated genealogy of the three Ita-kei gods'' (written according to the Nihon Shoki) {{familytree/start|style=font-size:100%}} {{familytree|border=0||||01| 01={{ill|lt=Susano-o|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susa no Takashi}}

({{ill|lt=Itaukeso Shrine|Q11379271|WD=Q11379271}})|02={{jalink|Ooyatsuhime Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|{{color|#FC4E6B|Oyatsuhime no Mikoto}}}}

As the company name suggests, it is said to be a shrine that enshrines '''{{ill|lt=Ooyatsuhime/Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|WD=Q10935047|lt=Tsumatsuhime}}''' (Tsumatsuhime no Mikoto, Sakutsuhime no Mikoto) as its enshrined deity.Regarding Tsumatsuhime, it is described in one book in "{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935}}" that she is the daughter god of {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo}}. Also, the older brother god of Tsumatsuhime is {{ill|lt=Isotakejin|Q10881556|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru/Itakeru}}, and the older sister god is {{ill|lt=Ooyatsuhime/Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|WD=Q10935047|lt=Oyatsuhime no Mikoto}} ( Both gods are enshrined at {{ill|lt=Itaikiso Shrine|Q11379271|WD=Q11379271}} (Itaikiso, Wakayama City) and {{ill|lt=Oyatsuhime Shrine|Q11434556|WD=Q11434556}} (Udamori, Wakayama City), respectively. These three deities are said to be the gods who brought tree species to Kii Province, and in Kii they are collectively known as ``''Itaiki Sosanjin'' {{Sfn|Takazumi Shrine (Taira)|1983}}. === History === ==== Outline ==== The first reference to the historical material is the article in "{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399}}" {{ill|lt=Taiho (Japan)|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taiho}} 2nd year ({{ill|lt=702|Q23497|WD=Q23497}})"Shoku Nihongi" article dated February 22, 702. In , there is an article about the separation of the three shrines of Ita-Kiso, Oya-Tohime, and Toma-Tohime.{{Sfn|Takazumi Shrine (Taira)|1983}}. "Shinsho Kaku Chofusho" {{ill|lt=Daido (Japan)|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daido}} 1st year ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021}})"Shinsho Kakuchofusho" 10 (Shinji Shoke Fudo) Daido 1st year (806). In , 13 houses were given to ``Tomazuhime no Kami'' of Kii Province {{Sfn|Takazumi Shrine (Taira)|1983}}. Related, {{ill|lt=Shohei (Japan)|Q1324874|WD=Q1324874|lt=Shohei}} ({{ill|lt=931|Q24789|WD=Q24789}}-{{ill|lt=938|Q24794|WD=Q24794}}) "{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Japanese name Ruijusho|UNKNOWN}}', ``Tsuma (Tsuma) Go'' is seen in Kii Province {{ill|lt=Nakusa District|Q11415547|WD=Q11415547}}, which is said to mean ``Tsuma Kobe'' {{Sfn|Takazumi Shrine (Taira)|1983}}.As {{ill|lt=Divine|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025}}, {{ill|lt=Jōkan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōkan}} In the first year of the year ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897}}), he was promoted to the lower rank of Jushii (junior fourth rank) as ``Shinto Matsuhime'' along with Itagesojin and Ōyatsuhime no kami {{Sfn|Takazumi Shrine (Taira)|1983}}. {{ill|lt=Extension (era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Extension}} 5 years ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}) "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}" {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shinmyocho}} states that in Nagusa District, Kii Province, there are "Tomatohime Shrine Meishindai It will be listed at {{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}} as ``Monthly New Year'' and {{ill|lt=Monthly Festival|Q11516161|WD=Q11516161}} and {{jalink| It is written that it was kept in {{ill|lt=heihaku|Q11481885|WD=Q11481885}} at Niinamesai|Q11501518}}{{Sfn|Takazumi Shrine (Taira)|1983}}. In ``{{ill|lt=Kiokuni Shinmecho|Q17210008|WD=Q17210008}}'', the Tenjin is listed as ``Junior Fourth Rank Upper Tumatsu Hime Okami'', and it is different from Itaukeso Okami, who rose to Shoichi Rank (Shoichi Rank), and has an open shrine {{Sfn|Takazumi Shrine (Taira)|1983}}. In addition, in the same book, there is also a related entry of ``Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Tsumutohime no Okami.'' Its subsequent history is unclear, and there are various theories as to which shrine the above shrine belongs to. ==== God level ==== * Records of {{ill|lt=Divine rank|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025}} dedication in the history of the Six Kingdoms ** {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan}} On the 27th of the first month of the first year ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897}}), the rank of Jufifth Rank, Lower Rank, Eighth Grade to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Rank, Eighth Grade (``{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}'') - The notation is ``Shinto Matsuhime God''. * After Six Kingdoms History ** Upper Juichii (``{{ill|lt=Kiokuni Shinto Name Book|Q17210008|WD=Q17210008}}'') - Written as ``Tomato Hime Okami.'' Is it a typo in "Junior Fourth Rank Upper"? === Historical evidence === The exact location of Shikinai Shrine ``Tomatsu Hime Shrine'' is still uncertain. In ancient times, it is identified as Miyako Matsuhime Shrine (Kichirei, (1)) in Shandong in the "Kiokuni Famous Places Zue" and "Nanki Shrine Record" ({{ill|lt=Enkyo|Q1183728|WD=Q1183728}}3 ({{ill|lt=1746|Q7589|WD=Q7589}})). Furthermore, in ``{{ill|lt=Kii Zoku Fudoki|Q11604964|WD=Q11604964}}'', it is said that Matsuhime Shrine (1), the capital of Shandong, which is mentioned in ``Nanki Shrine Roku'', was originally dedicated to ``Yoshire Tsuhime no Mikoto'' {{Sfn|Takazumi Shrine (Taira)|1983}}. Yoshiretsuhime-no-Mikoto appears in the ``Kiokuni Jinmyocho'' as ``Junior Fifth Rank Lower, Yoshiretsu Hime-no-Kami.'' In addition, in "Zoku Fudoki", there is a theory that Hirao's wife Daimyo Shrine (currently Tomatohime Shrine, (2)) is referred to as ``Toumatsuhime Shrine'' {{Sfn|Takazumi Shrine (Taira)|1983}}. One of these theories is that {{ill|lt=Water theory|Q2464727|WD=Q2464727}} ({{ill|lt=Water theory of Szene-Kunikake Shrine and Kodaimyojin|Q11504966|WD=Q11504966}}) that occurred between {{ill|lt=Maezen Shrine/Kokukake Shrine|Q888184|WD=Q888184}} and Wasaso. |Q1193059}}5 ({{ill|lt=1433|Q6356|WD=Q6356}}) states that Takazumi Shrine (3) was originally located in Maemiya, and later moved its enshrined place to Shandong, and later to Mt. Wasa.{{Sfn|Takazumi Shrine (Taira)|1983}}. Since this legend is consistent with an article written in 702 in the ``Shoku Nihongi'', ``Zoku Fudoki'' states that there is a high possibility that Takazumi Shrine is Tomatohime Shrine {{Sfn|Takazumi Shrine (Taira)|1983}}. === Miyakomatsuhime Shrine (Yoshire, Wakayama City) === {{Shrine |Name = Miyakomatsuhime Shrine |Image = [[File:Tsumatsuhime-jinja (Kire) shaden.JPG|280px]]
